channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Collier scores 26 to help Lynx top Sun 90-81 in Game 3 of semifinal series and take 2-1 lead
item.description: UNCASVILLE, Conn. (AP) — Napheesa Collier scored 26 points and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Connecticut Sun 90-81 to take a 2-1 lead in the best-of-five semifinal series Friday night. Game 4 is Sunday in Connecticut with the Lynx looking to advance to the WNBA Finals for the first time since 2017.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shohei Ohtani is feeling excitement, not nerves, on eve of his postseason debut with the Dodgers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is about to realize his childhood dream of playing in important games. There’s nothing bigger than the Major League Baseball playoffs. And the Japanese superstar isn’t nervous.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Padres hit with 'sudden and drastic' blow to starting rotation on eve of postseason series with Dodgers
item.description: The San Diego Padres are gearing up to try win their first World Series in franchise history - but they will have to do it without Joe Musgrove.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Businessman convicted of cheating ex-NBA star Dwight Howard out of millions in scam to buy WNBA team
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Georgia businessman was convicted Friday of scamming former NBA star Dwight Howard out of millions of dollars in a bogus scheme to buy the WNBA's Atlanta Dream. The jury returned its verdict against Calvin Darden Jr. on Friday afternoon after a trial in New York City.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GM David Forst expects payroll increase as A’s head to Sacramento
item.description: David Forst said during his end-of-season media availability that he expects the A's to be active in free agency and for the team's payroll to increase in 2025
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Devils beat Sabres 4-1 in NHL regular-season opener in Prague
item.description: Jacob Markstrom made 30 saves and Paul Cotter had an empty-netter and assist in their debuts with New Jersey as the Devils beat the Buffalo Sabres 4-1 in the NHL regular-season opener that was played in the Czech Republic capital.
item.dc:creator: Karel Janicek, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mets play broadcaster's viral call of Pete Alonso's home run on plane, give him standing ovation
item.description: Legendary Mets broadcaster Howie Rose had perhaps the best call of his career Thursday. When the team played it on the plane, players game him a standing ovation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mets' win sends ticket prices soaring for playoff matchup with Phillies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- Pete Alonso's three-run homer sent the New York Mets to a showdown with division rival Philadelphia -- and playoff ticket prices soaring.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: A deadly hurricane is the latest disruption for young athletes who already have endured a pandemic
item.description: Pisgah High School in western North Carolina reopened its football stadium last year after Tropical Storm Fred tore through in 2021. Now it has to be rebuilt again after being demolished by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top-ranked Sinner 'very confident' despite WADA appealing his doping case
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — Top-ranked tennis player Jannik Sinner is “very confident” he will avoid a doping ban after the World Anti-Doping Agency appealed a decision to clear him of wrongdoing following two positive drug tests.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landmark ruling from EU's top court says some FIFA rules on transfers are contrary to the bloc's law
item.description: The European Union's top court said Friday that some FIFA rules on player transfers are contrary to European Union legislation relating to competition and freedom of movement, in a ruling that will likely lead to a shakeup of the soccer market's regulations and could change the sport's economy.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Britain is back in America’s Cup final for the first time in 60 years
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — A British yacht is back in the America’s Cup finals for the first time in sixty years, and the island nation steeped in nautical heritage is one step away from ending the longest wait in international sports.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Buccaneers’ Vita Vea celebrates sack with sniper crawl amid NFL crackdown on 'violent' gestures
item.description: Amid the NFL's crackdown on "violent" gestures, Tampa Bay Buccaneers defensive tackle Vita Vea appeared celebrate a sack on Thursday night with a questionable gesture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NHL legend Henrik Lundqvist reflects on Johnny Gaudreau's death: 'Such a tragedy'
item.description: Hockey Hall of Famer and New York Rangers legend Henrik Lundqvist discussed the death of Johnny Gaudreau in a recent interview with Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees playoff tickets cost more in Kansas City than the Bronx
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- So, not everything is more expensive in New York City. Yankees fans will shell out less cash for a seat at Yankees Stadium than Kauffman Stadium in Kansas City when the American League Division Series kicks off Saturday at Yankee Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Padres beat Braves to advance to NLDS
item.description: The Padres booked their ticket to the National League Division Series on Wednesday, defeating the Atlanta Braves 5-4 in Game 2 of the Wild Card Series at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers issue statement on Fernando Valenzuela's status
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster and former player Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized and will miss the playoffs this year, but the team is hopeful he will return for next season.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Bucs look to build on division lead against the Falcons on Thursday Night Football | Bucs Bonus
item.description: Tampa Bay is looking to build on their early division lead during their Thursday Night Football matchup against the rival Atlanta Falcons.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: RSL looks to solidify playoff seeding against Minnesota
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake has already clinched an MLS playoff spot, but considering RSL is 6-1-2 all-time at home in the postseason, they want that home field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sitake wants to keep BYU hungry during bye week
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off a 34-28 win at Baylor, #17 BYU is 5-0 for the first time in three years. But head coach Kalani Sitake is going to make sure his team keeps its underdog mentality during the bye week. "That's our job," Sitake said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Pete Rose speaks with KTLA 5 in final interview
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball's all-time leader in hits and one of baseball's most divisive figures, died Monday at his home in Las Vegas. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Mets reach playoffs, beat Braves to cap comeback from 22-33 start
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Four months after dropping 11 games under .500, the New York Mets earned a playoff berth a day after the regular season was supposed to end.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: More brain injuries will occur from contact sports: Doctor
item.description: Brett Favre's diagnosis with Parkinson's disease has brought discussions of the long-term effects of contact sports on brain function to the fore.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Utah Royals beat Racing Lousiville 1-0
item.description: In their third-to-last home game of the season, Utah Royals FC beat Racing Louisville 1-0 off of a goal from Ana Tejada. There were chances from both sides during the match, but only one shot made it into the back of the net on the night.
item.dc:creator: Stockton Mair, ABC 4
